title: Five Habits That Sabotage Your Behavior Change
subtitle: 改变行为总失败？这五个习惯可能是罪魁祸首

paragraphs.en: According to recent research, behavioral change involves physical changes in the brain. In the past decade, researchers have shown that there is not a simple answer to how long it takes to make a new behavior a deep-rooted habit. Even for the most disciplined among us, undoing something automatic takes more than an overnight effort.
paragraphs.zh: 根据最新研究，行为改变涉及大脑的物理变化。过去十年间，研究人员已表明，将新行为变成根深蒂固的习惯需要多长时间，这个问题并没有简单答案。即便是我们中最自律的人，消除一项已经成为自身一部分的自动化行为，也绝非一日之功。

paragraphs.en: Once we've successfully made that change, we then have to make other adjustments to ensure that we continue to maintain it, which is often a whole other challenge in itself. At its core, success in changing and maintaining a behavior rarely occurs without the introduction of some sort of system. To ensure success, we should stay away from some detrimental habits.
paragraphs.zh: 一旦成功做出改变，我们还得进行其他调整以确保能维持下去，这本身往往又是另一项巨大挑战。从根本上看，若不引入某种系统，就很难成功改变并维持行为。为确保成功，我们应远离一些有害的习惯。

paragraphs.en: The first one to avoid is relying on willpower. Think about the last time you vowed to resist a temptation. Perhaps you were determined not to check your phone every 15 minutes or not to reach for a chocolate bar at 3 p.m. The research on whether we have finite or infinite willpower is inconclusive, but experts agree you can't change a habit if you rely on willpower alone.
paragraphs.zh: 第一个要避免的是依赖意志力。回想一下你上次发誓要抵制诱惑是什么时候。也许你决心不每15分钟看一次手机，或者不在下午三点去拿巧克力棒。关于意志力是有限还是无限的研究尚无定论，但专家一致认为，若只靠意志力，你无法改变和维持一个习惯。

paragraphs.en: The old military saying applies to behavior change: you repeat something so many times that it becomes automatic. Think about what else you can change about your surrounding that makes it easier to perform this change daily. This is called your "cue" — a trigger to perform that particular habit.
paragraphs.zh: 一句古老的军事格言同样适用于行为改变：你将某事重复足够多次，它就会变得自动。想一想你还能改变周围环境的哪些方面，使得每天执行这一改变更容易。这就是你的“提示”——一个触发你执行特定习惯的信号。

paragraphs.en: If you don't want to reach for a sugary treat at 3 p.m., have a box of herbal tea ready at your desk. When 3 p.m. comes around, that's your cue to pour yourself a cup and drink that tea, instead of walking to the vending machine. The second one to avoid is focusing on negative goals.
paragraphs.zh: 如果你不想在下午三点吃甜食，那就在桌上备好一盒花草茶。下午三点一到，这个时间点就是提示你给自己泡杯茶喝的信号，而不是走向自动售货机。第二个要避免的是专注于消极目标。

paragraphs.en: Not eating chocolate is a harder goal than swapping chocolate for herbal tea. Your brain wants to find routines that have succeeded in the past and allow you to repeat those actions without thinking. However, this habit-learning system isn't so effective when it comes to learning not to do something.
paragraphs.zh: 不吃巧克力比用花草茶代替巧克力更难实现。你的大脑想要找到过去成功的固定程式，并允许你在无需思考的情况下重复那些行动。然而，当涉及学习“不做某事”时，这种习惯学习系统就没那么有效了。

paragraphs.en: That's why rather than giving up something, think about introducing something in its place. Focus on actions you are going to take that will ultimately conflict with the behaviors you want to stop. The third one to avoid is using the same strategies in different circumstances.
paragraphs.zh: 正因如此，与其放弃某样东西，不如考虑引入另一样东西来替代它。专注于你将要采取的行动，这些行动最终会与你想要停止的行为相冲突。第三个要避免的是在不同情况下使用相同的策略。

paragraphs.en: Because we are creatures of habit, it's natural to assume that when we manage to sustain a desirable behavior, that same strategy will work for another change. But that's not always the case. Sometimes, the system that got you to change one behavior might not work for another.
paragraphs.zh: 由于我们是习惯的产物，我们很自然地会认为，当我们设法维持了一种理想行为时，同样的策略也能用在另一个改变上。但情况并非总是如此。有时候，让你成功改变一种行为的系统，可能对另一种行为无效。

paragraphs.en: Sometimes we rely on our guts when it comes to decision-making, which can hinder us when we need to consider data rather than letting instinct override everything. For example, if you want to stop checking email first thing in the morning, you might substitute another activity. But if you want to stop indulging in video games, simply deciding to go for a run might not be as effective.
paragraphs.zh: 有时我们做决定时习惯于凭直觉，这在我们需要参考数据而非让本能主导一切时，可能会成为阻碍。例如，如果你想改掉早上第一件事查邮件的习惯，你或许会用另一项活动来替代。但如果你想戒掉沉迷电子游戏，仅仅决定去跑步可能就没那么有效了。

paragraphs.en: The fourth one to avoid is not forgiving ourselves for slipping up. Even the best-laid plans fail sometimes. You might have stuck to your screen-free nighttime routine for five days, and then a big project landed on your desk and you found yourself in bed with your laptop.
paragraphs.zh: 第四个要避免的是不肯原谅自己的失误。即使是最周密的计划有时也会失败。你或许已经坚持了五天睡前不看屏幕的习惯，然后一个大项目落在桌上，你发现自己又抱着笔记本电脑上了床。

paragraphs.en: Life happens and even if your behavior change is small, every single day can prove inflexible. The perfectionist in you might be screaming to abandon your goals, but try to see it in the bigger picture. Just because you temporarily strayed off course doesn't mean you can't start afresh the next day.
paragraphs.zh: 生活总有意外，即使你的行为改变很小，每一天也可能充满变数。你内心的完美主义可能在尖叫着让你彻底放弃目标，但试着从更大的格局来看待它。仅仅因为你暂时偏离了轨道，并不意味着你第二天不能重新开始。

paragraphs.en: The final one to avoid is discounting small progress. Many perfectionists focus too much on the big goal and don't take time to celebrate the small progress they make. Your brain responds to rewards. The basal ganglia, linked to habit performance, is most active when the habit is cued and when it's rewarded.
paragraphs.zh: 最后一个要避免的是轻视微小的进步。许多完美主义者过于关注宏大目标，而没花时间去庆祝过程中取得的小进步。你的大脑会对奖励做出反应。与习惯执行相关的基底神经节，在习惯被提示时和得到奖励时最为活跃。

paragraphs.en: Rather than focusing on how far you've gone toward your goal, think about how you can reward yourself for the progress you've made. It doesn't have to be big; it can be something as simple as making your favorite juice after your run. Whatever your reward, it has to be more than just the activity itself.
paragraphs.zh: 与其关注自己离目标还有多远，不如想想如何为已取得的进步奖励自己。奖励不必很大，可以简单到跑完步后给自己榨一杯最喜欢的果汁。无论你的奖励是什么，它必须不仅仅是活动本身。

paragraphs.en: Initiating a new behavior usually seems like the hardest part. However, people often fail to adequately prepare for maintaining it. One reason is we mistakenly believe the strategies used to initiate the change will be equally effective in helping us continue it. But they won't.
paragraphs.zh: 启动新行为通常看似是最困难的部分。然而，人们往往未能为维持该行为做好充分准备。原因之一是我们错误地认为，用于启动变革的策略，在帮助我们持续变革时同样有效。但它们并非如此。

paragraphs.en: Where changing a deep-rooted habit requires changing our belief about that habit, continually manifesting that wisdom requires a positive outlook. If our mood is low, the wisdom to behave differently seems to disappear. The key, then, to maintaining new behaviors is to be happy.
paragraphs.zh: 改变根深蒂固的习惯需要改变我们对这个习惯的信念，而持续展现这种智慧则需要保持积极的心态。如果我们情绪低落，那种改变行为的智慧似乎就消失了。因此，维持新行为的关键在于保持快乐。

paragraphs.en: Remember, overcoming the behavioral inertia that prevents us from implementing new changes can benefit us in the long run. No one was born with habits. They were all learned, and can all, therefore, be unlearned. The question is: how badly do you really want to change?
paragraphs.zh: 请记住，克服阻碍我们实施新改变的行为惯性，从长远来看对我们有益。没有人天生就带有习惯。它们都是后天习得的，因此也都可以被遗忘。问题是：你到底有多想改变？
